title: Full-Stack Developer | .NET · React · Next.js · TypeScript
description: based in Montréal, Canada. Builds multi-tenant SaaS applications and REST APIs end-to-end with .NET/C# and React/Next.js, backed by 5+ years of professional QA experience and AI-assisted development workflows with Claude Code CLI and Atlassian MCP.
myworkd: These are some of the projects I've built.

billcrest.p1: Billcrest is a multi-tenant SaaS finance dashboard built over 9 months — 53 routes across 11 modules with tenant-scoped routing, 132 reusable TypeScript components, and a scalable design system with zero-flash dark/light theming. It consumes a .NET backend on Fly.dev through an OpenAPI-generated, fully type-safe TypeScript client, with role-based permissions and complete auth flows (login, register, password reset, email verification). Tested with ~1,500 test cases across Vitest, Testing Library, and axe-core for accessibility.
billcrest.header2: What can you do in this app?
billcrest.modify: Role-based account and tenant settings, backed by full auth flows including password reset and email verification.
billcrest.manage: Manage data across the platform's modules with paginated, tenant-scoped views.
billcrest.crud: CRUD: create, read, update, and delete records across the dashboard's domains through a type-safe API client.
billcrest.charts: Visual dashboards and reporting that surface trends across tenant data at a glance.
billcrest.invoice: Financial workflows covering the day-to-day operations the dashboard was built to manage.

moviesearch.p1: This is a small web-based application that allows users to search for information about movies, such as movie titles and release date. The app retrieves data from an API(The Open Movie DataBase, OMDb) and displays the results in a user-friendly format.
moviesearch.li1: Use the hook useRef to avoid doing the same request to the API
moviesearch.li2: Manipulate and manage data retrieved from APIs for faster retrieval.
moviesearch.li3: Implement search algorithms that can handle various search queries and filters.
moviesearch.li4: Work with third-party APIs to retrieve movie data.
moviesearch.li5: Optimize app performance for faster loading times and smoother user experience.
moviesearch.li6: Paginate data that can help break the data into smaller chunks that can be loaded as needed.

timelinedes: Timeline illustrating my progress and growth in learning.

timeline.college: Acquired the technical skills and knowledge required to develop high quality dynamic websites and web applications.
timeline.coursera: This course provided me a comprehensive and structured learning path to acquire the skills and knowledge necessary to become a proficient web developer.
timeline.billcrest: Building a multi-tenant SaaS finance dashboard end-to-end — a type-safe TypeScript client against a .NET API, a 130+ component design system, and full auth/RBAC flows — while managing the delivery lifecycle through Claude Code CLI, Jira, and automated testing with Vitest and Testing Library.
timeline.rizos: Built a multi-tenant appointment and business management REST API in ASP.NET Core, with a generic CRUD base controller, EF Core domain modeling, Firebase JWT authentication, and tenant context resolved per request. Deployed to Azure App Service.
timeline.moviesearch: Through my learning and experimentation, I broadened my range of skills in paginating data, and I became proficient in using useRef to enhance the search functionality and optimize HTTP requests.
timeline.mysql: Currently learning about how to create a database and make connection to a restful API.
timeline.node: Currently learning about how to create a simple restful API using Node and Express with the best practices.
timeline.keywords: Executing functional, regression, and exploratory testing across multiple software titles, managing the full defect lifecycle with Jira and TestRail, and partnering with developers and producers to triage defects and improve release stability.
timeline.ecommerce: E-commerce website based on the Abercrombie web page created using Typescript and Next. Also taking this opportunity to learn and practice new features of Next, like App Router.

ecommerce.p1: It's an e-commerce platform, allowing customers to browse and purchase their products online. Users can visit the website, browse through collections of products, select the items they wish to purchase, and add them to their shopping cart.
